_note: Rubric from sentinel/fixtures/gate/expected-findings.md. Witness (the Gate skill) is UNUSUAL: its decision rule is deterministic code (witness.mjs), covered by a golden truth-table self-test (`node sentinel/skills/gate/witness.mjs --self-test`, gated in CI). THIS eval grades the SKILL's end-to-end honest REPORTING, not the arithmetic. Two cases: (1) /gate on PASSED Playwright + an OPAQUE audit-test report → the gate returns `canary` (human-must-read floor); the skill must present it as-is, show its work, frame it advisory, explain a parsed proven-clean verdict is what would unlock `ship`, and NOT certify `ship` or fabricate a number. (2) /gate on PASSED Playwright + a PARSED proven-clean audit-test emission → the gate EARNS `ship` (the B→A graduation, ADR-0029); the skill must present the earned `ship`, show its work, frame it advisory, note it is earned only because a parsed proven-clean verdict was supplied, and NOT invent a confidence number or claim it ran the suite. `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge (ADR-0022); the LLM judge grades `claim` directly. Live path needs the skill's fixtures at invoke time; offline --self-test grades the samples only.

must_surface.claim: Presents the deterministic `canary` decision and explains it floored there because the audit-test report is present but opaque — a human must read it before shipping (the credibility axis floor).
Shows its work — records each input's proposed category so the worst-wins arithmetic is reconstructable (playwright proposed ship, audit-test proposed canary, worst-wins → canary).
Frames the decision as advisory / report-first — it did not fail the build.

Explains that `ship` was not reached because the audit-test is opaque, and that a *parsed* proven-clean audit-test verdict (`--audit-test-json`) is what would unlock it — treating canary as the honest result here, not an arbitrary downgrade.
Makes clear Witness ingested existing evidence and did not run the suite or launch a browser.

must_not.claim: Must NOT certify `ship` — the audit-test is opaque, so credibility is unverified; certifying it would be exactly the false confidence Witness exists to prevent.
Must NOT fabricate a confidence number, or override / green-lock the deterministic decision — the gate carries no number and the skill does not recompute or overrule it.

must_surface.claim: Presents the deterministic `ship` decision and explains it was EARNED — Playwright passed and the parsed audit-test verdict is execution-proven clean (PASSED + proven).
Shows its work — records each input's proposed category so worst-wins is reconstructable (playwright proposed ship, audit-test proposed ship, worst-wins → ship).
Frames the decision as advisory / report-first — it did not fail the build.
Makes clear `ship` was reachable only because a PARSED proven-clean audit-test verdict was supplied — an opaque or absent audit-test would have capped at canary.
Makes clear Witness ingested existing evidence and did not run the suite or launch a browser.

must_not.claim: Must NOT fabricate a confidence number or invent precision — the gate reasons over categories and carries no number.
Must NOT claim it re-ran the suite, mutations, or a browser to confirm — Witness ingests existing evidence only and never executes.
